rulings.Case Title: EUGENE GIFFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Turner Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence showing a triable issue whether Mr. Gifford was exposed to any asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. Adjudication of the false representation cause of action and punitive damages claim are off calendar, as they have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAN BUSBY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Zinklahoma, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion was untimely filed because it did not allow 2 additional days for electronic service. Since the motion was e-served on July 1, the earliest date for a hearing on the motion is September 16. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD SANDY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Although plaintiff requested and was granted a continuance to oppose the motion and represented that he had the evidence to do so, he never filed an opposition to the motion. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TINA LUPO et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corporation¿s motion to strike is off calendar as moot in light of the tentative ruling on the demurrer. For guidance of the parties in the future and without ruling on issues not currently before the court, once there is sufficient allegation of the product(s), the allegations relating to punitive damages for the survivorship claims most likely withstand a motion to strike, while the allegations relating to punitive damages for the wrongful death claims most likely do not. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TINA LUPO et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corporation¿s demurrer is sustained as to all challenged claims with ten days leave to amend. There is insufficient product identification. (Bockrath). For guidance of the parties in the future and without ruling on issues not currently before the court, once there is sufficient allegation of the product(s), the claims for negligence, breach of implied warranty and fraud/failure to warn most likely withstand a demurrer, while the claims for conspiracy most likely do not. As to conspiracy, see Chavers, 107 Cal. App. 4th 606). =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM BETTENCOURT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'S Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s memorandum of costs is granted as to the Berry & Berry costs and denied as to the Record Trak. The Berry & Berry costs are unrecoverable attorney and paralegal fees. The Record Trak costs are awarded in the court¿s deiscretion per CCP 1033.5(c). =(220/HEK)
